name: memorydetective
description: iOS leak hunting + performance investigation. Reads .memgraph and .trace files, classifies retain cycles against a 36-pattern catalog (SwiftUI / Combine / Concurrency / UIKit / Core Animation / Core Data / SwiftData / Coordinator / RxSwift / Realm). v1.10 closes the notelet retro feedback loop: reference-tree parser normalizes `<ClassName 0xADDR> [size]` per-address forms, framework-noise filter surfaces app-level + AV + KVO classes in new abandonedMemorySuspects / actionableGrowth / actionableShrinkage views, KVO co-occurrence classifier guards drop false positives, and `outputFormat: verify-fix-table` emits a focused 4-column markdown comparison table directly. v1.9 added analyzeAbandonedMemory for the leakCount=0 family, detectLeaksInXCTest per-test CI gate with self-contained HTML reports, cleanupTraces, and mainThreadViolations classification on analyzeHangs. Every classification ships with a Swift fixTemplate snippet. Single-call build + boot + launch with MallocStackLogging fixes the macOS 26.x leaks --outputGraph regression; replayScenario + captureScenarioState close the verify-fix loop. Bridges to Swift source via SourceKit-LSP. 34 MCP tools, 34 catalog resources, 5 investigation prompts.
